probes.documented_seam: Hermes top-level -z/--oneshot mode in hermes_cli/main.py and hermes_cli/oneshot.py
probes.probe_command: pnpm dev hermes probe execution.cli_headless --allow-run --hermes-bin scripts/hermes-contained-cli-probe.sh --hermes-home "$PWD/.cache/tc-hermes-cli-headless" --cwd "$PWD" --prompt "Reply with exactly HERMES_OK_CODEX_SUB" --out artifacts/hermes/probes/execution-cli-headless.json
probes.expected_result: Wrapper launches pinned Hermes through the contained script, receives the proof token, and records Docker inspect plus relay server-observed peer evidence
probes.negative_probe: Wrapper rejects forbidden env keys, credential-like env values, credential-like argv values, host-gateway relay routes, loopback/public peers, stale runtime digests, and runtime evidence not bound to the contained network

probes.documented_seam: Hermes MCP bridge tools events_wait, permissions_list_open, and permissions_respond in mcp_serve.py
probes.expected_result: Approval request is observed, responded to, and the same Hermes run continues without cross-thread leakage
probes.negative_probe: Wrong actor, stale request, replayed approval, and mutated decision are denied

probes.documented_seam: Pinned Hermes API server runs as a contained process behind Telclaude relay control
probes.expected_result: Contained Hermes API server starts with ephemeral bearer auth, advertises required run/approval/stop capabilities, and proves relay-only network containment
probes.negative_probe: Direct provider, vault, model-provider, private DNS, firewall flush, route add, and privileged runtime tamper attempts fail closed

probes.documented_seam: Telclaude-served Hermes MCP relay-only HTTP endpoint
probes.expected_result: HTTP JSON-RPC client proves positive tools-only control and specific adversarial denials
probes.negative_probe: Forged handle, wrong connection, cross-domain memory, out-of-scope provider/outbound, sampling, malformed, unauthenticated, batch, prototype-key, and execute-without-ledger calls fail closed

probes.documented_seam: Hermes model provider and endpoint configuration is generated by the wrapper and mounted read-only
probes.probe_command: pnpm dev hermes probe model.relay --allow-run --posture contained-internal --relay-url http://telclaude:8790/v1/models --model-url 'https://chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex/models?client_version=1.0.0' --profile-dir /home/hermes/.hermes --container-name tc-hermes-contained --expected-peer-address $TELCLAUDE_HERMES_CONTAINED_IP --relay-peer-address $TELCLAUDE_HERMES_RELAY_IP
probes.expected_result: Default model traffic uses the Telclaude relay-owned ChatGPT/Codex subscription path and reaches only relay endpoints; direct non-relay model-provider egress fails
probes.negative_probe: A planted model-provider plugin, writable profile override, raw OpenAI API key, or mounted Hermes/Codex OAuth file cannot bypass the relay route

probes.documented_seam: Telclaude WhatsApp edge adapter mediates ingress, outbound delivery, status, attachments, and credential custody before Hermes sees sanitized events.
probes.expected_result: WhatsApp inbound events reach the correct Hermes public or household profile as sanitized edge events and outbound replies use prepared Telclaude delivery refs only.
probes.negative_probe: Unknown senders, unscoped household provider requests, direct WhatsApp bridge access, raw session files, and Hermes-owned WhatsApp credentials fail closed.

probes.documented_seam: Telclaude identity migration binds Telegram, WhatsApp, email, AgentMail, social, and household actors to stable wrapper-owned actor and conversation refs.
probes.expected_result: Actor and conversation refs preserve source channel, trust domain, scopes, revocation state, and routing without giving Hermes authority to mint identity.
probes.negative_probe: Forged actor refs, stale/revoked identities, session-ID-as-authorization, and cross-channel identity escalation fail closed.

probes.documented_seam: Telclaude household scope policy maps family contacts to explicit allowed tasks, release policies, provider strong links, and revocation state before Hermes execution.
probes.expected_result: Household actors can complete benign scoped tasks and strong-link provider reads only when contact, consent, provider account, and recipient policy match.
probes.negative_probe: Phone-number-only provider access, private operator memory access, cross-recipient sends, and unscoped sensitive actions fail closed.

probes.documented_seam: Telclaude attachment quarantine stores raw channel uploads outside Hermes and passes only sanitized attachment refs through the edge adapter contract.
probes.expected_result: WhatsApp, email, AgentMail, browser, and computer attachments are represented to Hermes as quarantine refs with hashes, media metadata, and policy state only.
probes.negative_probe: Raw bytes, host local paths, download URLs, executable attachments, cross-domain reuse, and unscanned uploads fail closed.

probes.documented_seam: Telclaude outbound policy prepares, redacts, budgets, approves, executes, audits, and revokes outbound sends for WhatsApp, email, AgentMail, and social channels.
probes.expected_result: Hermes can request prepared outbound refs, but only Telclaude policy can authorize final channel delivery with recipient, thread, body, attachment, and budget binding.
probes.negative_probe: Hermes-supplied authority, transport credentials, policy results, approval tokens, mutated bodies, wrong recipients, and replayed outbound refs fail closed.

probes.documented_seam: Telclaude email edge adapter mediates mailbox ingress, threading, attachments, outbound send/reply, and credential custody before Hermes sees sanitized events.
probes.probe_command: pnpm dev hermes probe edge.email --allow-run --out artifacts/hermes/probes/edge-email.json
probes.expected_result: Email inbound/thread events reach the correct Hermes public or household profile as sanitized edge events and outbound mail uses prepared Telclaude delivery refs only.
probes.negative_probe: Wrong-thread replies, private-memory access from household mail, direct IMAP/SMTP access, and raw mailbox credentials fail closed.

probes.documented_seam: Telclaude AgentMail adapter or sidecar owns AgentMail polling/webhooks and API keys while Hermes receives sanitized mail events and prepared outbound refs.
probes.expected_result: AgentMail events and sends flow through Telclaude custody with allowed-sender policy, thread mapping, and no direct production key in Hermes.
probes.negative_probe: Direct AgentMail key use, unauthorized sender routing, thread forgery, and Hermes process access to AgentMail credentials fail closed.

probes.documented_seam: Telclaude social edge adapter owns public-social credentials, budgets, timeline ingestion, posting approvals, and audit before Hermes sees sanitized public-social events.
probes.probe_command: pnpm dev hermes probe edge.social --allow-run --out artifacts/hermes/probes/edge-social.json
probes.expected_result: Timeline, draft post, reply, and DM events route to an isolated Hermes public-social profile and approved outbound posts use Telclaude prepared refs only.
probes.negative_probe: Private-memory leakage, budget bypass, unapproved posting, credential exposure, and cross-domain social access fail closed.

probes.documented_seam: Telclaude public-social isolation runs social workflows in separate profile, memory namespace, network policy, credential custody, and posting budget authority.
probes.expected_result: Public-social workflows can use public social context while private workspace, provider scopes, private memory, and operator credentials remain unreachable.
probes.negative_probe: Private memory reads, private workspace mounts, provider scope use, social budget bypass, and silent private-to-public context bleed fail closed.

probes.documented_seam: Telclaude provider release policy decides whether a scoped actor, trust domain, conversation, recipient, and requested provider account may receive provider data or prepared actions.
probes.expected_result: Provider read and prepare flows release only allowed non-secret provider data to the bound actor/domain/conversation and audit the decision.
probes.negative_probe: Wrong actor, wrong recipient, missing strong link, emergency/urgent health misclassification, private-memory leakage, and unapproved sensitive release fail closed.

probes.documented_seam: Telclaude bank provider sidecar remains credential owner; Hermes accesses bank reads and prepared actions only through Telclaude MCP/provider proxy tools.
probes.expected_result: Balances, transactions, statements, transfer draft, and approved transfer execution work through sidecar custody, release policy, ledger binding, and audit.
probes.negative_probe: Wrong actor, replay, mutated transfer params, direct bank/provider/vault access, and raw credential exposure fail closed.

probes.documented_seam: Telclaude side-effect ledger and approval-token verifier bind provider writes to immutable prepared refs, actor, approver, profile, provider account, service/action, params/body hashes, TTL, and JTI.
probes.expected_result: Provider write execution succeeds only with a valid one-time approval token matching the immutable prepared action reference.
probes.negative_probe: Missing token, wrong actor, wrong service/action, params mutation, expiry, revocation, duplicate JTI, and replay fail closed.

probes.documented_seam: Telclaude-served Hermes MCP exposes only tc_provider_read, tc_provider_prepare_write, and tc_provider_execute_write behind relay-owned authority and connection context.
probes.expected_result: Served MCP provider tools stamp wrapper-derived authority, deny unsupported methods/resources/prompts/sampling, and route provider operations through Telclaude custody.
probes.negative_probe: Client-supplied authority, forged handles, sampling, direct resources/prompts, unregistered connections, out-of-scope providers, and execute-without-ledger fail closed.

probes.documented_seam: Telclaude Clalit/health provider sidecar remains credential owner; Hermes accesses health reads and prepared actions only through Telclaude MCP/provider proxy tools.
probes.expected_result: Appointments, lab/referral/medication reads, message drafts, booking prepare/execute, emergency escalation, and audit work through sidecar custody and release policy.
probes.negative_probe: Wrong actor, replay, emergency mishandling, direct Clalit/provider/vault access, and raw credential exposure fail closed.

probes.documented_seam: Telclaude government provider sidecar remains credential owner; Hermes accesses status/document reads and prepared submissions only through Telclaude MCP/provider proxy tools.
probes.expected_result: Status lookup, document fetch, form draft, approved submission, and audit work through sidecar custody, release policy, and signed write approvals.
probes.negative_probe: Wrong actor, replay, mutated submission params, direct government/provider/vault access, and raw credential exposure fail closed.

probes.documented_seam: Telclaude Google sidecar remains OAuth credential owner; Hermes accesses Gmail, Calendar, Drive, and Contacts only through Telclaude MCP/provider proxy tools.
probes.expected_result: Gmail/Calendar/Drive/Contacts reads, draft replies/events, approved writes, wrong-actor denial, replay denial, and direct provider/vault denial pass end to end.
probes.negative_probe: Mismatched approval-token audience, wrong actor, replay, mutated write params, direct Google/provider/vault access, and raw OAuth exposure fail closed.

probes.documented_seam: Telclaude cron/background scheduler remains workflow authority while Hermes execution receives bounded workflow run context and audited callbacks.
probes.expected_result: Scheduled delivery preserves workflowRunId, initiating actor, authority actor, scope, budget, freshness deadline, notification, and rollback-visible state.
probes.negative_probe: Duplicate scheduling, stale run execution, wrong actor authority, budget bypass, and invisible rollback state fail closed.

probes.documented_seam: Telclaude long-running workflow controller owns cancellation, freshness, approval waits, idempotent resume, and rollback visibility for Hermes-backed runs.
probes.expected_result: Long-running runs can pause for approval, resume idempotently with immutable context, handle provider timeouts, and remain cancellable and rollback-visible.
probes.negative_probe: Stale resume, replayed continuation, lost cancellation, mutated context, and hidden side effects fail closed.

probes.documented_seam: Hermes approval continuation is proven through the Telclaude MCP approval fallback using events_wait, permissions_list_open, permissions_respond, side-effect ledger, verifier, and JTI replay prevention.
probes.expected_result: Approval request is observed, authorized, and the same prepared side effect continues without cross-thread, wrong-actor, replay, or stale-decision leakage.
probes.negative_probe: Wrong actor, stale request, replayed approval, mutated decision, and unrelated run continuation are denied.

probes.documented_seam: Telclaude MCP side-effect ledger owns provider and outbound prepared refs, immutable hashes, status transitions, revocation, expiry, idempotency, and execution authorization.
probes.expected_result: Prepared provider/outbound refs authorize exactly one matching execution and record pending, approved, executed, revoked, and expired states in audit-safe form.
probes.negative_probe: Execute without ledger, kind mismatch, authority mismatch, expired/revoked/executed refs, params mutation, and replay fail closed.

probes.documented_seam: Telclaude browser broker owns isolated browser profiles, cookie jars, display/session custody, download/upload quarantine, and domain-specific target policy for Hermes web tasks.
probes.expected_result: Allowed public research works from isolated profiles while private, public-social, household, and provider domains cannot share cookies or workspace-backed browser state.
probes.negative_probe: Cross-domain cookie reuse, private profile leakage, forbidden target access, raw download exposure, and profile escape fail closed.

probes.documented_seam: Telclaude computer-use broker owns target authorization, display/session isolation, input/output audit, upload/download quarantine, and sidecar custody for sensitive sites.
probes.expected_result: Authorized computer targets work through audited broker sessions without giving Hermes unrestricted desktop or credential access.
probes.negative_probe: Unauthorized targets, cross-profile screen access, sensitive-site credential custody bypass, unquarantined upload/download, and covert egress fail closed.

probes.documented_seam: Telclaude egress broker applies browser/computer target policies, relay-only provider/model paths, metadata/RFC1918 denial, upload/download quarantine, and covert-egress controls.
probes.expected_result: Allowed public web egress succeeds only through the broker while direct SMTP/IMAP, WhatsApp bridge, model, vault, provider, metadata, and private-network egress fail.
probes.negative_probe: Direct private/provider/model/vault hosts, DNS exfiltration, unauthorized uploads, browser-to-provider bypass, and computer-use covert egress fail closed.
